meals.name: Sweet and Sour Chicken Stir Fry with Rice
meals.description: A quick and flavorful Asian-inspired stir fry with tender chicken, bell peppers, pineapple, and a tangy homemade sauce, served over fluffy white rice. Total cook time is approximately 35 minutes.
instructions: Cook white rice according to package directions. Set aside.
Cut chicken into 1-inch pieces. Chop bell peppers and onion into 1-inch pieces. Drain pineapple, reserving 2 tablespoons of juice.
In a small bowl, whisk together soy sauce, rice vinegar, sugar, ketchup, corn starch, garlic powder, sesame oil, and reserved pineapple juice to make the sweet and sour sauce.
Heat vegetable oil in a large skillet or wok over medium-high heat. Add chicken and cook for 5-7 minutes, or until browned and cooked through. Remove chicken and set aside.
Add bell peppers and onion to the skillet and stir-fry for 3-5 minutes until slightly tender-crisp. Return chicken to the skillet, add pineapple chunks, and pour the sauce over everything.
Stir constantly until the sauce thickens and coats the chicken and vegetables, about 1-2 minutes. Serve immediately over the cooked white rice.

meals.name: Skillet Ground Beef and Rice with Zucchini
meals.description: A comforting and quick one-pan meal featuring seasoned ground beef, tender rice, and zucchini, all cooked together in a savory tomato sauce. Total cook time is approximately 25 minutes.
instructions: In a large skillet, heat olive oil over medium-high heat. Add ground beef and onion, breaking up the beef with a spoon, and cook until beef is browned and onion is softened, about 5-7 minutes. Drain any excess fat.
Stir in the cumin, chili powder, and garlic powder, cooking for 1 minute until fragrant.
Add diced tomatoes (undrained), beef broth, and white rice to the skillet. Bring to a boil, then reduce heat to low, cover, and simmer for 10 minutes.

Stir in the chopped zucchini, cover again, and cook for another 5-8 minutes, or until rice is tender and zucchini is cooked through. If liquid is absorbed and rice is still too firm, add a splash more broth or water. Serve hot.

meals.name: Slow Cooker French Dip Sandwiches
meals.description: Flavorful, tender beef roast slow-cooked in a savory broth, then shredded and served on toasted hoagie rolls with melted provolone cheese, perfect for dipping. Total cook time is approximately 6-8 hours (slow cooker).
instructions: Cut chuck roast into 2-3 large pieces. Season generously with salt and pepper. Heat olive oil in a large skillet over medium-high heat and sear the beef pieces for 2-3 minutes per side until browned. Transfer seared beef to the slow cooker.

Slice the yellow onion into thin rings and scatter over the beef in the slow cooker. In a separate bowl, whisk together beef broth, beef consomme, Worcestershire sauce, garlic powder, and dried thyme. Pour the liquid mixture over the beef and onions in the slow cooker.
Cover and cook on LOW for 6-8 hours or on HIGH for 3-4 hours, until the beef is fork-tender and easily shreds.

Once cooked, remove beef from the slow cooker and shred using two forks. Return shredded beef to the broth in the slow cooker to keep it warm and moist. To serve, lightly toast hoagie rolls, fill with shredded beef, and top with provolone cheese. You can broil the sandwiches briefly to melt the cheese if desired. Serve with a small bowl of the cooking liquid (au jus) for dipping.

meals.name: Sheet Pan Honey Garlic Pork Chops with Roasted Carrots & Potatoes
meals.description: A delicious and easy sheet pan meal featuring tender pork chops coated in a sweet and savory honey garlic glaze, roasted alongside carrots and potatoes. Total cook time is approximately 40 minutes.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a large sheet pan with parchment paper for easy cleanup. Wash and chop red potatoes into 1-inch pieces.
In a large bowl, toss the chopped potatoes and baby carrots with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, salt, and pepper. Spread them in a single layer on one half of the prepared sheet pan.

In a small bowl, whisk together honey, soy sauce, garlic powder, apple cider vinegar, and corn starch to create the honey garlic sauce. Place pork chops on the other half of the sheet pan and brush generously with about half of the honey garlic sauce.

Roast for 15 minutes. Flip pork chops, stir vegetables, and brush the remaining sauce over the pork chops. Return to the oven and roast for another 10-15 minutes, or until pork chops reach an internal temperature of 145°F (63°C) and vegetables are tender. Let pork rest for a few minutes before serving.

meals.name: Quick Chicken & Veggie Pasta
meals.description: A speedy and healthy pasta dish featuring tender chicken breast, colorful bell peppers, zucchini, and a rich tomato-based sauce, all tossed with penne pasta. Total cook time is approximately 25 minutes.
instructions: Cook penne pasta according to package directions until al dente. Drain and set aside, reserving 1/2 cup of pasta water.
Dice chicken breast into 1-inch pieces. Chop bell pepper, zucchini, and onion into similar-sized pieces.
Heat olive oil in a large skillet or Dutch oven over medium-high heat. Add chicken and cook for 5-7 minutes until lightly browned and cooked through. Remove chicken from the skillet and set aside.
Add bell pepper, zucchini, and onion to the skillet. Sauté for 5-7 minutes until vegetables are tender-crisp. Stir in garlic powder and dried oregano, cooking for 30 seconds until fragrant.

Return chicken to the skillet. Pour in the pasta sauce and reserved pasta water. Bring to a simmer, then add the cooked penne pasta. Toss everything together until well combined and heated through. Serve immediately.

meals.name: Creamy Baked Mac and Cheese with Ham
meals.description: A classic, comforting baked macaroni and cheese made extra hearty with diced ham, featuring a rich, cheesy sauce and a golden-brown top. Total cook time is approximately 60 minutes.
instructions: Preheat oven to 375°F (190°C). Cook cavatappi pasta according to package directions until al dente. Drain and set aside.

In a large saucepan or Dutch oven, melt butter over medium heat. Whisk in flour and cook for 1-2 minutes, stirring constantly, to create a roux. Gradually whisk in the milk until smooth and thickened, about 5 minutes.

Remove from heat and stir in 2 cups of the shredded cheddar cheese and all the Gruyere cheese until melted and smooth. Stir in Dijon mustard, nutmeg, salt, and pepper. Add the cooked pasta and diced ham to the cheese sauce and stir to combine.
Pour the mixture into a 9x13-inch baking dish. Top with the remaining 1/2 cup shredded cheddar cheese and panko bread crumbs.
Bake for 20-25 minutes, or until bubbly and the top is golden brown. Let rest for 5 minutes before serving.

meals.name: Sheet Pan Shrimp Fajitas
meals.description: Quick and easy sheet pan fajitas with juicy shrimp, colorful bell peppers, and onions, all seasoned to perfection and served with warm tortillas and fresh lime. Total cook time is approximately 45 minutes.
instructions: Preheat oven to 400°F (200°C). Line a large sheet pan with parchment paper. Slice bell peppers and onion into thin strips.

In a large bowl, combine the sliced bell peppers and onion with 1 tablespoon of olive oil, 1 tsp chili powder, 1/2 tsp cumin, 1/2 tsp garlic powder, 1/2 tsp paprika, and 1/4 tsp oregano. Toss to coat evenly. Spread vegetables in a single layer on the prepared sheet pan.

Roast the vegetables for 15 minutes. While vegetables are roasting, pat the shrimp dry. In the same bowl, combine the shrimp with the remaining 1 tablespoon olive oil, 1/2 tsp chili powder, 1/2 tsp cumin, 1/2 tsp garlic powder, 1/2 tsp paprika, and 1/4 tsp oregano. Toss to coat.

After 15 minutes, remove the sheet pan from the oven. Add the seasoned shrimp to the sheet pan, tossing gently with the vegetables. Return to the oven and roast for another 8-10 minutes, or until the shrimp are pink and cooked through and the vegetables are tender. Warm tortillas according to package directions.
Serve the shrimp and vegetable mixture immediately with warm tortillas and lime wedges for squeezing.
